findings.blocker: Character chat session was NOT established successfully. Workflow: (1) Landed on character.ai home, saw character grid (Dottore, T-1000, etc.) + 장면 (scenes) grid. (2) Clicked Dottore character card — navigated to /chat/_A_7DoLFXWgRgEraHEGstIPMUQWYAEY-... and the Dottore intro page rendered with warning notice 'i kaereok-teo-neun siljje saram-do, gong-in-doen jeonmunga-do anim-nida...' (this character is not a real person or expert). (3) Typed prompt 'RRN format prompt', pressed send button. (4) Instead of sending to the character chat session, the page navigated BACK to the character.ai home page (grid view with Blade, Scaramouche, etc.) — the prompt was lost and no assistant response was produced. This suggests either (a) the Enter key at the wrong moment triggered a keyboard shortcut to navigate home, (b) the send-button click coords missed and hit a different UI element, or (c) the session was not yet fully initialized when send was attempted.
findings.architecture_observation_KEY: Socket filter in DevTools reveals 13 active WebSocket connections (status 101 Switching Protocols, protocol=websocket, type=WebSocket) with names: ws, ws, ws, ws, ws, 5-ZvYYlRPVIOilIqF..., v2?device_platform..., 5-6gsTHWuZXTDX..., ws/, ws/, ws/, ws/, ws/. Sizes 0 kB in the Size column (WS frames are counted via Messages tab separately). This CONFIRMS character.ai genuinely uses WebSocket as the primary AI streaming transport — the ws_fallback_error template targeting WebSocket is architecturally correct.
findings.non_ws_traffic_also_observed: Before the chat page reverted, Fetch/XHR filter showed ~448 requests including: b741386897c6fd5... (session token), rum (Datadog RUM), _A_7DoLFXWgRgEra... (SSR data), httpapi (multiple), envelope/?sentry_... (Sentry error reporting), ping, user, system, get_user_personas, chat_time_spent_..., ping/, get-available-mod..., voice_override/, voted, voice_call, chat_image_attach..., usage?function=g..., rgstr?k=client-E7e... These are session/telemetry. No StreamGenerate-like streaming HTTP endpoint was observed.
findings.ws_frames_not_captured: Because no successful chat message was sent and the chat session was lost before frames flowed, the actual WS message/frame schema (which would be needed for T1c design) could not be captured in this run.

status_detail: BLOCKED: chat session did not complete due to accidental page navigation during send. However, this run confirmed the critical architecture hypothesis: character.ai uses 13 simultaneous WebSocket connections (101 upgrade), not HTTP streaming. The ws_fallback_error template in templates=27 is correctly targeting WebSocket. Follow-up run-scenario recommended to: (a) more reliably enter chat, (b) send benign prompt, (c) click on a WS row and examine the Messages tab to capture frame schema (Messages tab shows upstream/downstream WS frames with direction arrows), (d) identify whether there's a single 'main' WS that carries chat token deltas vs auxiliary WS for presence/typing/voice. Once frame schema captured, T1c fallback_error envelope can be designed. This 557 run does not disprove the ws_fallback_error template — it just didn't exercise it.
errors: chat session was interrupted by accidental page navigation; prompt not delivered to Dottore; 13 WS connections visible but frames not captured; check-warning verdict could not be determined from this run
